Example 96 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 96
Source Text
Common warts (verrucae vulgaris) are caused by HPV types 1, 2, 4, and 7 and occasionally other types in immunosuppressed patients (eg, 75 to 77). They are usually asymptomatic but sometimes cause mild pain when they are located on a weight-bearing surface (eg, bottom of the feet).
Generated Simplification
### Common Warts: What You Need to Know - **What are common warts?** Common warts, also called verrucae vulgaris, are small growths on the skin. They are caused by certain types of the human papillomavirus (HPV), specifically types 1, 2, 4, and 7. - **Who can get them?** Most people can develop common warts. However, people with weakened immune systems may sometimes get them from other HPV types as well. - **Do they cause symptoms?** Common warts usually do not cause pain or other symptoms. - If a wart is on a weight-bearing area, like the bottom of your foot, it might cause mild pain when you walk or stand. If you have concerns about warts or notice pain, talk to your healthcare provider. They can help you understand your options for treatment or management.
Reference Simplification
Most warts are painless, but some hurt when touched. Warts on the feet can cause pain when standing or walking.

Example 97 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 97
Source Text
Found fossilized fauna were numerous organisms previously thought to have died out after the mid-Cambrian.
Generated Simplification
The discovery of fossilized animals shows that many organisms, once believed to have gone extinct after the mid-Cambrian period, actually survived longer than previously thought.
Reference Simplification

Example 101 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 101
Source Text
Dementia also occurs in patients with Parkinson disease, Huntington disease, progressive supranuclear palsy, Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease, Gerstmann-StrÃ¤ussler-Scheinker syndrome, other prion disorders, and neurosyphilis.
Generated Simplification
Dementia can happen in people with certain medical conditions. These include: - Parkinson’s disease - Huntington’s disease - Progressive supranuclear palsy - Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease - Gerstmann-Sträussler-Scheinker syndrome - Other prion diseases (a group of rare brain disorders) - Neurosyphilis (a serious brain infection caused by untreated syphilis) If you have one of these conditions or are concerned about dementia, talk to your doctor. They can help explain your risks and discuss any next steps.
Reference Simplification
Disorders that can cause dementia include the following: Parkinson disease (a common cause); Brain damage due to a head injury or certain tumors; Huntington disease; Prion diseases, such as Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease (which causes a rapidly progressive dementia); Progressive supranuclear palsy; Neurosyphilis.

Source Text
Ehrlichiosis and anaplasmosis may result in disseminated intravascular coagulation, multiorgan failure, seizures, and coma.
Generated Simplification
Ehrlichiosis and anaplasmosis are serious infections. They can lead to dangerous complications, including: - A condition called disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC), which affects blood clotting. - Failure of multiple organs in the body. - Seizures. - Coma. If you have symptoms of these infections or concerns about these complications, talk to your doctor right away.
Reference Simplification
Ehrlichiosis and anaplasmosis may cause widespread blood clotting (disseminated intravascular coagulation), severe malfunction (failure) of several organs, seizures, and coma.

Encephalitis is a condition that affects the brain. Example 108 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 108
Source Text
Endoscopy with biopsy
Generated Simplification
### Endoscopy with Biopsy: What You Need to Know **What is an Endoscopy?** An endoscopy is a procedure where a doctor uses a thin, flexible tube with a camera to look inside your body. This helps them see areas like your stomach, esophagus (the tube that connects your throat to your stomach), or intestines. **What is a Biopsy?** During an endoscopy, the doctor may take a small sample of tissue. This is called a biopsy. The tissue is sent to a lab to check for any problems, like inflammation, infection, or signs of disease. **Why is it Done?** Your doctor may recommend an endoscopy with a biopsy if: - You have symptoms like pain, trouble swallowing, or ongoing stomach issues. - They need to check for conditions such as ulcers, infections, or cancer. **What to Expect During the Procedure** - You will likely be given medicine to help you relax or sleep during the procedure. - The doctor will gently guide the tube into your body to examine the area. - If a biopsy is needed, it will be done quickly and should not cause pain. **After the Procedure** - You may feel a little sore or bloated, but this usually goes away soon. - Follow any instructions your doctor gives you, such as resting or avoiding certain foods for a short time. **Important Notes** - If you have questions about why you need this procedure, talk to your doctor. - Let your doctor know if you have any allergies or take medications, as this could affect the procedure. - If you notice unusual symptoms after the procedure, like severe pain or bleeding, contact your doctor right away. Always discuss your concerns and next steps with your healthcare provider.
Reference Simplification
Endoscopy and biopsy

Precision

Recall
0.241
F1
-0.137

Example 109 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 109
Source Text
Erythema marginatum is a serpiginous, flat or slightly raised, nonscarring, and painless rash. The rash usually appears on the trunk and proximal extremities but not the face. It sometimes lasts < 1 day.
Generated Simplification
### Understanding Erythema Marginatum - **What is it?** Erythema marginatum is a type of rash. It is flat or slightly raised, does not leave scars, and does not cause pain. - **Where does it appear?** This rash usually shows up on the chest, back, or upper parts of the arms and legs. It does not appear on the face. - **How long does it last?** The rash may sometimes go away in less than one day. If you notice a rash like this, it is a good idea to talk to your healthcare provider. They can help determine the cause and whether any treatment is needed.
Reference Simplification
A flat, painless rash with a wavy edge (erythema marginatum) may appear as the other symptoms subside. It lasts for only a short time, sometimes less than a day.

Example 116 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 116
Source Text
Immunodeficiency occurs when the immune system is less active than normal, resulting in recurring and life-threatening infections.
Generated Simplification
### What is Immunodeficiency? Immunodeficiency happens when your immune system does not work as well as it should. This makes it harder for your body to fight off infections. ### What Does This Mean for You? - You may get infections more often than usual. - These infections can sometimes be very serious or even life-threatening. If you think you might have symptoms of immunodeficiency, talk to your doctor. They can help figure out what’s going on and discuss treatment options with you.
Reference Simplification
Immunodeficiency diseases occur when the immune system is less active than normal.

Example 119 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 119
Source Text
By using the term adaptation for the evolutionary process, and adaptive trait for the bodily part or function ( the product ), one may distinguish the two different senses of the word. Adaptation is one of the two main processes that explain the observed diversity of species, such as the different species of Darwin 's finches.
Generated Simplification
Adaptation is a term used to describe how living things change over time to survive in their environment. - The process of change over time is called "adaptation." - A specific body part or function that helps a living thing survive is called an "adaptive trait." For example, the different types of beaks in Darwin's finches are adaptive traits. These changes help explain why there are so many different species in nature.
Reference Simplification
By using the term adaptation for the evolutionary process, and adaptive trait for the bodily part or function ( the product ), the two senses of the word may be distinguished.

### What This Means A sharp injury happens when something cuts deep enough to damage the second layer of your skin, called the dermis. ### Next Steps If you have a deep cut, it’s important to: - Clean the wound carefully to prevent infection. - Cover it with a clean bandage. - Watch for signs of infection, like redness, swelling, or pus. ### When to See a Doctor You should contact a healthcare professional if: - The cut is large or deep. - The bleeding doesn’t stop after applying pressure. - You notice signs of infection. Always follow your doctor’s advice for treating injuries.

Example 121 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 121
Source Text
Genitourinary syndrome of menopause includes symptoms and signs due to estrogen deficiency such as Vulvovaginal atrophy; Urinary urgency; Dysuria; Frequent UTIs.
Generated Simplification
**Understanding Genitourinary Syndrome of Menopause** Genitourinary syndrome of menopause is a condition that happens because of lower estrogen levels during menopause. It can cause several symptoms. These include: - **Vulvovaginal atrophy**: This means the tissues in and around the vagina become thinner, drier, or less elastic. This can cause discomfort. - **Urinary urgency**: You may feel a sudden, strong need to urinate. - **Dysuria**: This refers to pain or a burning feeling when you urinate. - **Frequent urinary tract infections (UTIs)**: You may get UTIs more often. If you notice any of these symptoms, it’s important to talk to your healthcare provider. They can help explain your options for managing these symptoms and improving your comfort.
Reference Simplification
Genitourinary syndrome of menopause is a new, more accurate term used to refer to symptoms that affect the vagina and urinary tract and that are caused by menopause, such as vaginal dryness, pain during sexual intercourse, urinary urgency, and urinary tract infections.

Example 123 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 123
Source Text
However, compared with Alzheimer disease, vascular dementia tends to cause memory loss later and to affect executive function earlier.
Generated Simplification
Vascular dementia and Alzheimer’s disease affect the brain in different ways. - In vascular dementia, memory problems often appear later. - However, issues with planning, decision-making, and problem-solving (called executive function) tend to happen earlier. If you have concerns about memory or thinking skills, talk to your doctor. They can help determine the cause and discuss next steps.
Reference Simplification
However, compared with Alzheimer disease, vascular dementia tends to cause memory loss later and to affect judgment and personality less. Vascular dementia tends to cause difficulty planning and initiating actions earlier than Alzheimer disease.

Initial symptoms of rabies are nonspecific: fever, headache, and malaise.
Generated Simplification
**Understanding Early Symptoms of Rabies** Rabies can start with symptoms that are not specific to the disease. These early signs may include: - Fever - Headache - Feeling generally unwell (this is called malaise). If you notice these symptoms and think you may have been exposed to rabies, contact a healthcare professional right away. Rabies is a serious condition, and early treatment is very important.
Reference Simplification
Rabies may begin with a fever, headache, and a general feeling of illness (malaise).

The Hammond-Leffler postulate suggests that this too will increase the rate of carbocation formation.

The Hammond-Leffler postulate is a scientific idea that explains how certain chemical reactions happen. It suggests that some changes can make carbocations form faster. Carbocations are unstable molecules that can appear during chemical reactions. If this topic is related to your health or treatment, please discuss it with your healthcare provider for more information.
Reference Simplification
The Hammond-Leffler postulate says that this too will increase the rate of carbocation formation.

Example 140 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 140
Source Text
Thalassemia can cause significant complications, including iron overload, bone deformities and cardiovascular illness.
Generated Simplification
Thalassemia is a blood condition that can lead to serious health problems. These may include: - Too much iron in the body (iron overload), which can damage organs. - Changes in the shape of bones (bone deformities). - Heart problems (cardiovascular illness). If you have thalassemia, talk to your doctor about how to manage these risks and protect your health.
Reference Simplification
Thalassemia can cause significant complications, including pneumonia, iron overload, bone deformities and cardiovascular illness.

Source Text
Lyme disease has 3 stages: Early localized; Early disseminated; Late.
Generated Simplification
Lyme disease happens in three stages: 1. **Early Localized Stage** This is the first stage of the disease. 2. **Early Disseminated Stage** This is the second stage, where the infection may spread to other parts of the body. 3. **Late Stage** This is the third stage, which can happen if the disease is not treated earlier. If you think you might have Lyme disease or have questions about these stages, talk to your doctor.
Reference Simplification
Lyme disease has three stages: Early localized; Early disseminated (widespread); Late.

Example 142 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 142
Source Text
The nervous system consists of a ventral nerve cord, with one ganglion in each segment, and an anterior nerve ring surrounding the pharynx.
Generated Simplification
### Understanding the Nervous System - The nervous system is made up of a ventral nerve cord. This is a main nerve that runs along the body. - Each body segment has one ganglion. A ganglion is a cluster of nerve cells that helps control movement and other functions. - At the front of the body, there is an anterior nerve ring. This ring surrounds the pharynx, which is the part of the throat that connects the mouth to the digestive system. If you have questions about how the nervous system works or how it relates to your health, talk to your healthcare provider.
Reference Simplification
It consists of a ventral nerve cord, with one ganglion in each segment, and an anterior nerve ring surrounding the pharynx.

A few months prior to the London Paralympics, he had problems with his tendons.

A few months before the London Paralympics, he experienced issues with his tendons. Tendons are the strong bands of tissue that connect muscles to bones. Problems with tendons can cause pain or make it harder to move. If you are having similar tendon issues, it’s important to talk to your doctor. They can help identify the cause and recommend treatment.
Reference Simplification
A few months before the London Paralympics, he had problems with his tendons.

Example 147 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 147
Source Text
Esophageal candidiasis is an opportunistic infection of the esophagus by Candida albicans.
Generated Simplification
**Esophageal Candidiasis** Esophageal candidiasis is an infection in the esophagus, which is the tube that connects your mouth to your stomach. This infection is caused by a type of fungus called *Candida albicans*. This fungus is normally present in the body, but it can cause problems if it grows too much. If you think you might have this condition, or if you have symptoms like trouble swallowing or pain in your throat or chest, talk to your doctor. They can help diagnose and treat it.
Reference Simplification
Esophageal candidiasis is an infection of the esophagus by Candida albicans.

Example 151 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 151
Source Text
Treprostinil can be given intravenously or subcutaneously, but the subcutaneous form can be very painful.
Generated Simplification
Treprostinil is a medication that can be given in two ways: - Through an intravenous (IV) line, which delivers the medicine directly into a vein. - Through a subcutaneous (under the skin) injection. However, the subcutaneous option can cause a lot of pain for some people. If you are considering this treatment, talk to your doctor about which method might be best for you.
Reference Simplification
Treprostinil ( Remodulin) can be given intravenously or subcutaneously, but the subcutaneous form can be very painful.

Example 158 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 158
Source Text
Myoclonus can vary in amplitude, frequency, and distribution.
Generated Simplification
Myoclonus refers to sudden, involuntary muscle jerks. - These jerks can differ in size. Some may be small, while others are more noticeable. - They can also happen at different speeds, either quickly or more slowly. - Myoclonus can affect one part of the body or several areas. If you experience muscle jerks, talk to your doctor. They can help determine the cause and discuss possible treatments.
Reference Simplification
Myoclonus can be mild or severe. Muscles may jerk quickly or slowly, rhythmically or not. Myoclonus may occur once in a while or frequently.

Example 162 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 162
Source Text
Nonconvulsive status epilepticus is being increasingly recognized as a cause of altered mental status in ICU patients.
Generated Simplification
### Nonconvulsive Status Epilepticus and Mental Changes in ICU Patients - Nonconvulsive status epilepticus is a type of seizure that does not cause visible shaking or convulsions. - It can lead to changes in mental state, such as confusion or unresponsiveness. - This condition is being identified more often as a reason for mental changes in patients in the intensive care unit (ICU). If you or a loved one is in the ICU and experiencing unusual mental changes, it is important to discuss this possibility with the healthcare team.
Reference Simplification
People in ICUs may have seizures that do not cause convulsions (called nonconvulsive seizures). These seizures can cause delirium, but the seizures may not be recognized because they do not cause convulsions or other typical symptoms of seizures. If the seizures are not recognized, they may not be treated appropriately and promptly.

Source Text
Gout can present in a number of ways, although the most usual is a recurrent attack of acute inflammatory arthritis ( a red, tender, hot, swollen joint ).
Generated Simplification
Gout can show up in different ways. The most common sign is a sudden attack of joint inflammation. This means the joint becomes: - Red - Tender to the touch - Warm or hot - Swollen If you notice these symptoms, talk to your doctor. They can help confirm if it’s gout and discuss treatment options.

Gout can present in a number of ways, although the most common is a recurrent attack of intense inflammatory arthritis ( a red, tender, hot, swollen joint ).

Example 164 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 164
Source Text
They include a wide variety of pathogens, such as Escherichia, Salmonella, Vibrio, Helicobacter, and many other notable genera.
Generated Simplification
There are many types of germs that can cause infections. These include: - **Escherichia** (a group of bacteria, including E. coli, which can sometimes cause illness). - **Salmonella** (a bacteria that can cause food poisoning). - **Vibrio** (a bacteria often linked to infections from contaminated water or seafood). - **Helicobacter** (a bacteria that can cause stomach problems, like ulcers). There are also other similar types of germs that can lead to infections. If you have concerns about infections or symptoms, talk to your healthcare provider.
Reference Simplification
Proteobacteria include a wide variety of pathogens, such as Escherichia coli, Salmonella, Vibrio, Helicobacter, and many other notable genera.

Example 165 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 165
Source Text
Familial Alzheimer 's disease and familial Parkinson 's disease are two accelerated aging diseases associated with aged individuals; they affect only one tissue and can be classified as unimodal progeroid syndromes.
Generated Simplification
**Familial Alzheimer’s Disease and Familial Parkinson’s Disease** - These are conditions linked to aging. - They are called "accelerated aging diseases" because they cause certain parts of the body to age faster than normal. - Each disease affects only one type of tissue in the body. - Doctors classify them as "unimodal progeroid syndromes," which means they involve early aging in just one specific area. If you have concerns about these diseases or think they may affect you or your family, talk to your healthcare provider. They can provide more information and guidance.
Reference Simplification
Familial Alzheimer 's disease and familial Parkinson 's disease, an accelerated aging disease associated with aged individuals, affects only one tissue: it can be classified as an unimodal progeroid syndrome.

Example 167 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 167
Source Text
Olfactory seizures, manifested as an aura of foul smells (rotten eggs, burnt meat), indicate temporal lobe involvement and suggest HSV encephalitis.
Generated Simplification
### Olfactory Seizures and Their Meaning - **What are olfactory seizures?** Olfactory seizures are a type of seizure where a person smells something unpleasant that isn’t actually there. Common examples include smells like rotten eggs or burnt meat. - **What do they indicate?** These seizures often involve a part of the brain called the temporal lobe. They can also suggest a condition called HSV encephalitis, which is a serious brain infection caused by the herpes simplex virus. - **What should you do?** If you experience these types of smells or think you might be having olfactory seizures, it’s important to talk to a healthcare professional right away. They can help determine the cause and recommend the right treatment.
Reference Simplification
People also have seizures, sometimes accompanied by strange smells (such as rotten eggs), vivid flashbacks, or sudden, intense emotions.

**What is an Endoscopy?** - An endoscopy is a medical procedure that allows doctors to look inside your body. - It is done using a tool called an endoscope. **What is an Endoscope?** - An endoscope is a thin, flexible tube with a light and camera. - It helps doctors see the inside of hollow organs or spaces in your body. If you have questions about this procedure or why it might be needed, talk to your doctor.

Endoscopy means to look inside and often means looking inside the body for medical reasons using an instrument called an endoscope.

Example 170 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 170
Source Text
Pain is typically in the perineum but may be perceived at the tip of the penis, lower back, or testes.
Generated Simplification
Pain is usually felt in the area between the anus and the genitals (called the perineum). However, some people may feel the pain in other areas, such as: - The tip of the penis - The lower back - The testes (the glands in the scrotum that produce sperm) If you are experiencing pain in any of these areas, it is important to talk to your healthcare provider. They can help determine the cause and recommend the right treatment.
Reference Simplification
Pain develops in the perineum, the lower back, and often the penis and testes.

Talk to your doctor about the best treatment for your situation.
Reference Simplification

Example 174 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 174
Source Text
Patients are taller than average for age and family; arm span exceeds height. Arachnodactyly (disproportionately long, thin digits) is noticeable, often by the thumb sign (the distal phalanx of the thumb protrudes beyond the edge of the clenched fist).
Generated Simplification
Some patients may be taller than expected for their age or compared to other family members. Their arm span (the distance from fingertip to fingertip when arms are stretched out) may also be longer than their height. A condition called arachnodactyly can be present. This means the fingers are unusually long and thin. One way to notice this is through the "thumb sign." In this sign, when the person makes a fist, the tip of their thumb sticks out past the edge of the fist. If you notice these features in yourself or someone else, it is important to discuss them with a healthcare professional. They can help determine if further evaluation is needed.
Reference Simplification
People with Marfan syndrome are taller than expected for their age and family. Their arm span (the distance between fingertips when the arms are outstretched) is greater than their height. Their fingers are long and thin.

Example 175 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 175
Source Text
Patients may present with systemic symptoms such as fever (usually low - grade), fatigue, malaise, unexplained weight loss, and sweats. Some patients are initially diagnosed as having fever of unknown origin.
Generated Simplification
You may experience general symptoms that affect your whole body. These can include: - A mild fever - Feeling very tired - A general sense of not feeling well - Losing weight without trying - Sweating, especially at night In some cases, doctors may not immediately know the cause of your fever. This is sometimes called a "fever of unknown origin." If you have these symptoms, talk to your doctor. They can help figure out what might be causing them and recommend the next steps.
Reference Simplification
People may have fever and feel tired and generally unwell. They may lose weight unintentionally and sweat more than usual.

Example 176 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 176
Source Text
Patients suspected of having stomach cancer should have endoscopy with multiple biopsies and brush cytology. Occasionally, a biopsy limited to the mucosa misses tumor tissue in the submucosa.
Generated Simplification
If your doctor suspects stomach cancer, they will likely recommend an endoscopy. This is a procedure where a thin, flexible tube with a camera is used to look inside your stomach. During the procedure, small tissue samples (biopsies) will be taken for testing. In some cases, the doctor may also use a technique called brush cytology. This involves collecting cells from the stomach lining with a small brush for further testing. It’s important to know that sometimes a biopsy might only take tissue from the surface layer of the stomach (the mucosa). If the cancer is deeper in the next layer (the submucosa), it might not be detected. If you have concerns or questions about these tests, talk to your doctor. They can explain the process and what the results might mean for you.
Reference Simplification
Endoscopy (an examination in which a flexible tube is used to visualize the inside of the digestive tract) is the best diagnostic procedure. It allows a doctor to view the stomach directly, to check for Helicobacter pylori, and to remove tissue samples for examination under a microscope (biopsy).

0.416

Example 179 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 179
Source Text
Seven days of the oral fluoroquinolone ciprofloxacin is typically used in areas where the resistance rate is less than 10 %.
Generated Simplification
In some areas, the antibiotic ciprofloxacin is used to treat infections. It is taken by mouth for seven days. However, this treatment is only recommended in places where less than 10% of bacteria are resistant to ciprofloxacin. You should talk to your doctor to find out if this treatment is suitable for you based on local resistance rates.
Reference Simplification
Seven days of the oral fluoroquinolone ciprofloxacin is typically used in geographic areas where the resistance rate is less than 10 %.

Source Text
The study also indicated that increased ejaculation through masturbation rather than intercourse would be more helpful as intercourse is associated with diseases ( STDs ) that may increase the risk of cancer instead.
Generated Simplification
A study suggests that ejaculating more often may be helpful for health. This benefit seems to come more from masturbation than from sexual intercourse. This is because sexual intercourse can sometimes lead to sexually transmitted diseases (STDs). STDs may increase the risk of certain cancers. If you have questions about this or your own health, it’s a good idea to talk with your healthcare provider.
Reference Simplification
The study also indicated that this would be more helpful than ejaculation through sexual intercourse because intercourse can transmit diseases that may increase the risk of cancer instead.

Example 181 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 181
Source Text
Waist circumference and BP
Generated Simplification
### Waist Circumference and Blood Pressure (BP) #### Waist Circumference - Waist circumference is the measurement around your waist. - It helps doctors understand how much fat is stored in your belly area. - Too much fat around your waist can increase your risk of health problems, like heart disease, diabetes, and high blood pressure. #### Blood Pressure (BP) - Blood pressure is the force of blood pushing against your blood vessels. - High blood pressure (also called hypertension) can damage your heart, kidneys, and other organs over time. - It is important to keep your blood pressure in a healthy range. #### How Waist Size and Blood Pressure Are Connected - A larger waist size is often linked to higher blood pressure. - This means that carrying extra weight around your belly can make it harder to keep your blood pressure under control. #### What You Can Do - **Measure Your Waist:** Ask your doctor how to measure your waist correctly. - **Check Your Blood Pressure:** Regularly check your blood pressure at home or at your doctor’s office. - **Healthy Lifestyle:** Eating healthy, staying active, and maintaining a healthy weight can help lower your waist size and blood pressure. - **Talk to Your Doctor:** If you are concerned about your waist size or blood pressure, speak with your doctor. They can give you advice and recommend steps to improve your health. #### Important Note - If your waist size or blood pressure is high, it’s important to take action. - Always follow your doctor’s advice and attend regular check-ups.
Reference Simplification
Waist circumference Blood pressure measurement

Example 184 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 184
Source Text
A palpable bladder may be present with outlet obstruction. The costovertebral angle may be tender if the kidney is acutely enlarged.
Generated Simplification
### Understanding Possible Symptoms - If there is a blockage preventing urine from leaving the bladder (called an outlet obstruction), the bladder may feel swollen or noticeable to the touch. - If the kidney becomes suddenly enlarged, the area on your back near the lower ribs (called the costovertebral angle) may feel tender or sore. ### What This Means for You - If you notice swelling or tenderness in these areas, it could be a sign of a problem with your bladder or kidneys. - These symptoms should be discussed with your healthcare provider. They can examine you and determine the cause.
Reference Simplification
If acute kidney injury is caused by a blockage (an obstruction), the backup of urine within the kidneys causes the drainage system to stretch (a condition called hydronephrosis â see Figure: Hydronephrosis: A Distended Kidney).

Example 185 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 185
Source Text
A peripheral ring scotoma (detectable by visual field testing) widens gradually, and central vision may also be affected in advanced cases.
Generated Simplification
A peripheral ring scotoma is a type of vision problem. It can be found during a visual field test. - Over time, this condition causes a gradual loss of vision around the edges of your sight (peripheral vision). - In more advanced stages, it may also affect your central vision, which is the area you see directly in front of you. If you notice changes in your vision, talk to your eye doctor. They can check for this condition and discuss treatment options if needed.
Reference Simplification
In the late stages of the disease, the person typically has only a small area of central vision and possibly no peripheral vision remaining (tunnel vision). Sometimes in late stages the macula, the area of the retina responsible for central vision, swells. This swelling is called macular edema and affects central vision.

Example 186 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 186
Source Text
for kids Bruises in sports
Generated Simplification
### Bruises in Sports When children play sports, they can sometimes get bruises. A bruise happens when small blood vessels under the skin break, usually from a bump or fall. This causes the skin to look discolored, often turning blue, purple, or yellow as it heals. #### What to Do for a Bruise - **Rest the area:** If the bruise is on a leg or arm, try to limit movement to avoid more pain or swelling. - **Apply ice:** Use a cold pack or a cloth with ice wrapped inside. Place it on the bruise for 10–15 minutes at a time. This can help reduce swelling and pain. - **Elevate the area:** If possible, raise the bruised area above the level of the heart. This can help reduce swelling. #### When to See a Doctor Most bruises heal on their own in a few days to a week. However, you should contact a doctor if: - The bruise is very large or painful. - The child has frequent bruises without clear reasons. - The bruise doesn’t improve after a week. - There is swelling or difficulty moving the area. - The child has other symptoms, like fever or unusual tiredness. #### Preventing Bruises in Sports - Make sure your child wears the right protective gear, like helmets, pads, or shin guards. - Teach your child to play safely and follow the rules of the sport. - Ensure they warm up and stretch before playing to reduce the risk of injuries. If you have concerns about your child’s bruises or their overall health, talk to your doctor.
Reference Simplification
for kids Bruises in sports Fake bruises as fashion

Example 187 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 187
Source Text
Monozygotic twins have a concordance of about 45 %.
Generated Simplification
**Understanding Monozygotic Twins and Concordance** - Monozygotic twins are also called identical twins. They come from the same fertilized egg and share the same genetic material. - "Concordance" means how often both twins have the same condition or trait. For monozygotic twins, the concordance rate is about 45%. This means that if one twin has a certain condition, there is a 45% chance the other twin will also have it. If you have questions about what this means for your health or family, talk to your doctor.
Reference Simplification
An identical twin whose co-twin has schizophrenia has about a 50 % risk of developing schizophrenia.

Example 188 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 188
Source Text
Findings characteristically include conjunctival edema and hyperemia and a discharge.
Generated Simplification
### Eye Symptoms to Be Aware Of Some common findings in certain eye conditions include: - **Conjunctival edema**: This means swelling in the thin, clear tissue that covers the white part of your eye. - **Hyperemia**: This refers to redness in the eye caused by increased blood flow. - **Discharge**: You may notice fluid or mucus coming from the eye. If you experience these symptoms, it’s important to talk to your healthcare provider. They can help determine the cause and recommend the right treatment.
Reference Simplification
The conjunctiva becomes red and sometimes swells, giving the surface of the eyeball a puffy appearance. The eyelids may become intensely itchy.

Example 189 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 189
Source Text
Advanced disease can lead to destruction of supporting cartilage, causing floppy ears, saddle nose, pectus excavatum, and visual, auditory, and vestibular abnormalities.
Generated Simplification
Advanced disease can damage the cartilage that supports certain parts of the body. This can lead to: - Floppy ears (ears that lose their firmness). - Saddle nose (a nose that looks flattened or sunken). - Pectus excavatum (a chest that appears sunken or hollow). - Problems with vision, hearing, or balance. If you notice any of these changes, talk to your healthcare provider. They can help determine the cause and discuss possible treatments.
Reference Simplification
Eventually, the supporting cartilage can be damaged, resulting in floppy ears, a sloping saddle nose, and a hollow at the lower part of the chest (pectus excavatum). The nerve in the inner ear can be affected, causing eventual problems with balance and hearing, and eventually vision problems can occur.

Example 191 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 191
Source Text
Nephritis is inflammation of the kidneys and may involve the glomeruli, tubules, or interstitial tissue surrounding the glomeruli and tubules.
Generated Simplification
Nephritis is a condition where the kidneys become inflamed. This inflammation can affect different parts of the kidneys, such as: - The glomeruli, which are tiny filters in the kidneys that help clean your blood. - The tubules, which are small tubes that help remove waste and extra fluid. - The interstitial tissue, which is the space around the glomeruli and tubules. If you have concerns about kidney health or symptoms like swelling, changes in urination, or pain, talk to your healthcare provider. They can help determine if nephritis is a concern and discuss next steps.
Reference Simplification
It may involve the glomeruli, tubules, or interstitial tissue surrounding the glomeruli and tubules. Nephritis is often caused by infections, and toxins, but is most commonly caused by autoimmune disorders that affect the major organs like kidneys. Nephritis can produce glomerular injury, by disturbing the glomerular structure with inflammatory cell proliferation.

Strabismus is a condition where the eyes do not look in the same direction. - Some people are born with it. This is called infantile strabismus. - Others develop it later in life. Strabismus may appear in the first few months of life or later in childhood, depending on the cause.

Example 193 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 193
Source Text
Nasogastric suction
Generated Simplification
### Nasogastric Suction: What You Need to Know **What is Nasogastric Suction?** Nasogastric suction is a medical procedure. It uses a thin, flexible tube called a nasogastric (NG) tube. This tube is inserted through your nose, down your throat, and into your stomach. The purpose is to remove fluids, air, or substances from your stomach. **Why is it Done?** Doctors may recommend nasogastric suction for several reasons: - To relieve pressure or bloating caused by a buildup of gas or fluids in your stomach. - To remove harmful substances, such as swallowed toxins or blood. - To help your stomach rest after surgery or an injury. - To prepare your stomach for certain medical tests or treatments. **How is it Done?** - A healthcare professional will gently insert the NG tube through your nose. - You may feel some discomfort or a gagging sensation during the process. - Once the tube is in place, it will be connected to a suction device to remove the unwanted contents. **What Should You Expect?** - The procedure is usually quick, but the tube may stay in place for a longer time if needed. - You might feel some irritation in your nose or throat while the tube is in place. - Let your healthcare provider know if you feel pain, discomfort, or if the tube seems blocked. **Are There Any Risks?** Nasogastric suction is generally safe, but there are some risks, including: - Nosebleeds or irritation in your nose and throat. - A small chance of the tube being misplaced. - Rarely, the tube may cause damage to your stomach or throat. **What Should You Do?** - Follow your doctor’s instructions carefully. - If you experience severe pain, difficulty breathing, or notice blood, tell your healthcare provider immediately. - Ask your doctor if you have questions about why the procedure is needed or how it will help you. Always discuss any concerns or symptoms with your healthcare provider. They are there to help you understand and manage your care.
Reference Simplification
Suction via nasogastric tube.

Example 194 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 194
Source Text
Symptoms and signs of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis typically develop over 6 mo to several years and include dyspnea on exertion and nonproductive cough.
Generated Simplification
### Symptoms of Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) is a lung condition that develops slowly over time. Symptoms usually appear over 6 months to several years. Common symptoms include: - **Shortness of breath during activity**: You may feel out of breath when walking, climbing stairs, or doing other physical tasks. - **Dry cough**: This is a cough that does not produce mucus or phlegm. If you notice these symptoms, it is important to talk to your healthcare provider. They can help determine the cause and recommend the next steps.
Reference Simplification
Symptoms depend on the extent of the lung damage, the rate at which the disease progresses, and whether complications, such as lung infections and right-sided heart failure (cor pulmonale) develop. The main symptoms start insidiously as shortness of breath during exertion, cough, and diminished stamina. In most people, symptoms worsen over a period ranging from about 6 months to several years.

Symptoms can last from 6 mo to > 10 yr and range from nonexistent to severe.
Generated Simplification
Symptoms can last for different lengths of time. - They may go away after 6 months. - In some cases, they can continue for more than 10 years. - The severity of symptoms can also vary. Some people may not notice any symptoms, while others may have very severe ones. If you are experiencing symptoms, talk to your healthcare provider. They can help you understand your situation and discuss possible next steps.

Example 197 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 197
Source Text
Symptoms include rectal itching, a cloudy rectal discharge, bleeding, and constipation âall of varying severity. Examination with a proctoscope may detect erythema or mucopurulent exudate on the rectal wall.
Generated Simplification
Symptoms you might experience include: - Itching around the rectum. - A cloudy discharge from the rectum. - Bleeding from the rectum. - Constipation. These symptoms can vary in how severe they are. To check for the cause of these symptoms, a doctor may use a tool called a proctoscope. This is a small device that helps examine the inside of the rectum. During the exam, the doctor might notice: - Redness (called erythema) on the rectal wall. - A thick, mucus-like discharge (called mucopurulent exudate). If you have these symptoms, it’s important to talk to your doctor. They can help figure out what’s causing them and recommend the right treatment.
Reference Simplification
This infection usually causes no symptoms, but it may make bowel movements painful. Other symptoms include constipation, itching, bleeding, and a discharge from the rectum. The area around the anus may become red and raw, and stool may be coated with mucus and pus. When a doctor examines the rectum with a viewing tube (anoscope), mucus and pus may be visible on the wall of the rectum.

Example 198 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 198
Source Text
Ascites, splenomegaly, or portosystemic encephalopathy may be present.
Generated Simplification
### Understanding Possible Conditions Some conditions that may occur include: - **Ascites**: This is when fluid builds up in the abdomen, causing swelling. It can make your belly feel tight or uncomfortable. - **Splenomegaly**: This means the spleen is enlarged. The spleen is an organ that helps filter blood and fight infections. - **Portosystemic Encephalopathy**: This is a condition that can affect the brain. It happens when toxins build up in the blood because the liver is not working properly. It may cause confusion, forgetfulness, or changes in behavior. ### What This Means for You If you notice symptoms like a swollen belly, confusion, or unusual tiredness, it’s important to talk to your doctor. These conditions may be related to liver problems or other health issues. ### Next Steps - Pay attention to any new or worsening symptoms. - Write down any changes you notice and share them with your healthcare provider. - Discuss these conditions with your doctor to understand if they apply to your situation and what steps to take next.
Reference Simplification
If a large amount of fluid accumulates in the abdomen, the abdomen swells (distends), sometimes noticeably and sometimes enough to make the abdomen greatly enlarged and taut. This distention can be uncomfortable or painful. An enlarged spleen may cause a vague sense of discomfort in the upper left part of the abdomen.

Example 200 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 200
Source Text
Obstetric ultrasonography should be done to rule out hydatidiform mole and multifetal pregnancy.
Generated Simplification
### What You Need to Know An **obstetric ultrasound** is a type of scan used during pregnancy. It helps doctors check the health of the pregnancy. ### Why Is This Test Important? Your doctor may recommend this test to: - **Rule out a hydatidiform mole**: This is a rare condition where abnormal tissue grows in the uterus instead of a normal pregnancy. - **Check for multifetal pregnancy**: This means checking if you are carrying more than one baby, such as twins or triplets. ### What Should You Do? - If your doctor suggests this test, it is important to follow their advice. - Ask your doctor if you have questions about why the test is needed or what the results might mean. ### Talk to Your Doctor If you are unsure about this test or have concerns, discuss them with your healthcare provider. They can explain how it applies to your situation.
Reference Simplification
Ultrasonography is usually done to determine whether women have a hydatidiform mole (a disorder that initially resembles pregnancy) or more than one fetus (multiple births).

Example 201 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 201
Source Text
IV fluids.
Generated Simplification
**Understanding IV Fluids** - **What are IV fluids?** IV fluids are liquids given directly into a vein through a small tube called an IV. "IV" stands for "intravenous," which means "into the vein." - **Why are IV fluids used?** IV fluids are used to help your body when you need extra fluids. This might happen if you are dehydrated, have lost blood, or need certain medicines or nutrients quickly. - **How are they given?** A healthcare professional will place a small needle into a vein, usually in your arm or hand. The needle is then connected to a tube that delivers the fluid. - **What should you know?** - IV fluids are safe when given by trained professionals. - You might feel a small pinch when the needle is inserted. - Let your healthcare provider know if you feel pain, swelling, or discomfort during the process. - **When should you ask questions?** If you are unsure why you need IV fluids or have concerns about the process, talk to your doctor or nurse. They can explain how IV fluids will help in your specific situation.
Reference Simplification
Fluids given by vein

Example 202 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 202
Source Text
The peripheral nervous system ( PNS ) is the part of the nervous system consisting of the nerves and ganglia outside of the brain and spinal cord.
Generated Simplification
The peripheral nervous system, or PNS, is a part of your body’s nervous system. It includes the nerves and small groups of nerve cells (called ganglia) that are located outside the brain and spinal cord. The PNS helps send messages between your brain, spinal cord, and the rest of your body.
Reference Simplification
The Peripheral nervous system, or PNS, is part of the nervous system, and consists of the nerves and neurons that are outside the central nervous system ( the brain and spinal cord ) and serve the limbs and organs, for example.

Example 203 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 203
Source Text
Bronchiectasis is best considered the common end-point of various disorders that cause chronic airway inflammation.
Generated Simplification
### What is Bronchiectasis? Bronchiectasis happens when the airways in your lungs become damaged and inflamed over time. This condition can develop as a result of different health problems that cause long-term swelling or irritation in the airways. If you think you might have symptoms of bronchiectasis, or if you have a condition that could lead to it, talk to your healthcare provider. They can help diagnose and manage it.
Reference Simplification
The most common cause is severe or repeated respiratory infections, which are particularly likely in people who have one or more of the following: Cystic fibrosis; Other hereditary disorders, such as primary ciliary dyskinesia, in which the ability to clear the airways is impaired; Immunodeficiency disorders; Certain autoimmune disorders, such as rheumatoid arthritis, ulcerative colitis, or Sjogren syndrome; Mechanical factors, such as airway blockage caused by an inhaled object, chronically enlarged lymph nodes, changes after lung surgery, or a lung tumor; Inhaling toxic substances that injure the airways, such as noxious fumes, gases, smoke (including tobacco smoke), and injurious dust (for example, silica and coal dust).

Example 206 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 206
Source Text
The United Nations ' FAO states that by 2025, 1.9 billion people will live in countries or regions with absolute water scarcity, and two-thirds of the world population could be under stress conditions.
Generated Simplification
By 2025, the United Nations' Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) predicts: - 1.9 billion people will live in areas with very little water (absolute water scarcity). - Two-thirds of the world’s population may face water shortages (stress conditions). This highlights the growing need to conserve water and use it wisely.
Reference Simplification
A water crisis is a situation where the available supply of potable, clean water within an area is less than the demand for it. The Food and Agriculture Organization ( FAO ) estimates that by 2025, 1.9 billion people will be living in countries or regions with total water scarcity, and two-thirds of the world population could be under stress conditions.

Example 207 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 207
Source Text
Pain occurs along the distribution of one or more sensory divisions of the trigeminal nerve, most often the maxillary.
Generated Simplification
Pain can happen in areas connected to one or more branches of the trigeminal nerve. This nerve is responsible for feeling in your face. The pain most commonly affects the maxillary branch, which is the part of the nerve that serves the middle part of your face, including your cheeks, upper jaw, and upper teeth. If you have concerns about this type of pain, talk to your healthcare provider. They can help identify the cause and discuss treatment options.
Reference Simplification
Pain due to trigeminal neuralgia can occur spontaneously but is often triggered by touching a particular spot (called a trigger point) on the face, lips, or tongue or by an action such as brushing the teeth or chewing.

Example 210 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 210
Source Text
A corneal ulcer begins as a corneal epithelial defect that stains with fluorescein and an underlying dull, grayish, circumscribed superficial opacity (representing an infiltrate).
Generated Simplification
A corneal ulcer is a problem that affects the clear, front part of your eye (the cornea). - It starts with a small injury or defect on the surface of the cornea. - This defect can be seen using a special dye called fluorescein, which highlights the damaged area. - Beneath this damaged spot, there may be a dull, grayish area that looks cloudy. This cloudy area is called an infiltrate. If you think you might have a corneal ulcer, it is important to see an eye doctor right away.
Reference Simplification
The ulcer often appears as a white or dull and grayish spot on the cornea.

Example 212 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 212
Source Text
From May to October 2000, an outbreak of tularemia in Martha 's Vineyard resulted in one fatality, and brought the interest of the CDC as a potential investigative ground for aerosolized Francisella tularensis.
Generated Simplification
In 2000, there was an outbreak of a disease called tularemia on Martha's Vineyard. This happened between May and October. Tularemia is caused by a bacteria called *Francisella tularensis*. During this outbreak, one person died. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) became involved to investigate. They were concerned that the bacteria might have spread through the air. If you have questions about tularemia or think you may have been exposed, talk to your healthcare provider.
Reference Simplification
It brought the interest of the CDC as a potential investigative ground for aerosolized Francisella tularensis.

Example 215 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 215
Source Text
90.9 % of these were classified as having been done by `` curettage '' (suction-aspiration, dilation and curettage, dilation and evacuation), 7.7 % by `` medical '' means (mifepristone), 0.4 % by `` intrauterine instillation '' (saline or prostaglandin), and 1.0 % by `` other '' (including hysterotomy and hysterectomy).
Generated Simplification
Most of these procedures (90.9%) were done using a method called "curettage." This includes techniques like suction-aspiration, dilation and curettage (D&C), or dilation and evacuation (D&E). A smaller number (7.7%) were done using "medical" methods. This means using medication, such as mifepristone, to complete the procedure. Only 0.4% were done by "intrauterine instillation." This involves placing a solution, like saline or prostaglandin, into the uterus. About 1.0% were done using "other" methods. These include surgeries like hysterotomy or hysterectomy. If you have questions about these methods or how they might apply to your situation, talk to your healthcare provider.
Reference Simplification
90.9 % were done by curettage, 7.7 % were medical abortions (using drugs, mifepristone in most cases), 0.4 % by `` intrauterine instillation '' (saline or prostaglandin), and 1.0 % by `` other '' (including hysterotomy and hysterectomy).

0.462

Example 216 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 216
Source Text
About 10 % of patients have dermatitis herpetiformis, an intensely pruritic papulovesicular rash that is symmetrically distributed over the extensor areas of the elbows, knees, buttocks, shoulders, and scalp.
Generated Simplification
Approximately 10% of patients may develop a skin condition called dermatitis herpetiformis. - This condition causes a very itchy rash with small bumps and blisters. - The rash usually appears on both sides of the body in specific areas. These areas include the elbows, knees, buttocks, shoulders, and scalp. If you notice a rash like this, talk to your healthcare provider. They can help diagnose and manage the condition.
Reference Simplification
About 10 % of people with celiac disease develop a painful, itchy rash with small blistersâa disease called dermatitis herpetiformis.

Example 217 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 217
Source Text
Anteroposterior, lateral, and usually oblique x-rays are taken. A fracture, if present, is usually visible on the lateral view. X-rays may be normal if only the tendon is torn.
Generated Simplification
### Understanding Your X-Ray Results - Doctors usually take three types of X-rays: front-to-back (anteroposterior), side view (lateral), and sometimes an angled view (oblique). - If you have a broken bone (fracture), it is often easiest to see on the side view (lateral X-ray). - However, if only a tendon (the tissue that connects muscle to bone) is torn, the X-rays might look normal. ### What This Means for You If your X-rays do not show a fracture but you still have pain or other symptoms, your doctor may consider other causes, like a tendon injury. Be sure to discuss your symptoms and next steps with your healthcare provider.
Reference Simplification
X-rays are usually taken at several different angles to check for a fracture.

Example 220 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 220
Source Text
Because the heterozygote survives , so does the HgbS allele survive at a rate much higher than the mutation rate ( see and refs in Sickle-cell disease ) .
Generated Simplification
The HgbS allele, which is linked to sickle cell disease, continues to exist at a higher rate than expected from new mutations alone. This happens because people who inherit one copy of the HgbS gene (called heterozygotes) are more likely to survive. If you have questions about how this might relate to your health or genetic risks, discuss this with your healthcare provider.
Reference Simplification
Because the heterozygote survives , so does the HgbS allele survive at a rate much higher than the mutation rate .

Example 222 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 222
Source Text
Crohn disease should be suspected in a patient with inflammatory or obstructive symptoms or in a patient without prominent GI symptoms but with perianal fistulas or abscesses or with otherwise unexplained arthritis, erythema nodosum, fever, anemia, or (in a child) stunted growth. A family history of Crohn disease also increases the index of suspicion.
Generated Simplification
### Understanding Crohn's Disease Crohn's disease is a condition that affects the digestive system. It can cause inflammation and other problems. Here are some signs and situations where Crohn's disease might be suspected: #### Common Symptoms - Problems like inflammation or blockage in the digestive system. - Perianal issues, such as fistulas (abnormal connections between tissues) or abscesses (painful, swollen areas filled with pus). #### Other Possible Signs - Unexplained joint pain or arthritis. - Red, painful skin lumps (called erythema nodosum). - Fever without a clear cause. - Anemia (low red blood cell levels). - In children, slower growth than expected. #### Family History - If someone in your family has Crohn's disease, your chances of having it may be higher. ### What to Do If you have any of these symptoms or concerns, talk to your doctor. They can help figure out if Crohn's disease might be the cause and discuss the next steps for testing or treatment.
Reference Simplification
A doctor may suspect Crohn disease in a person with recurring crampy abdominal pain and diarrhea, particularly if the person has a family history of Crohn disease or a history of problems around the anus.

Example 224 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 224
Source Text
However, many also go on to form abscesses, which require surgical drainage; an extensive procedure is done with accompanying IV antibiotics.
Generated Simplification
Sometimes, infections can lead to abscesses. An abscess is a pocket of pus that forms in the body due to infection. If this happens, the abscess may need to be drained through surgery. This is a more involved procedure. After the surgery, you may also need antibiotics given through an IV (a tube placed into a vein). If you think you might have an abscess, or if you are concerned about an infection, talk to your doctor right away.
Reference Simplification
Abscesses must be drained surgically.

Example 225 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 225
Source Text
In mammals, it consists of the bony labyrinth, a hollow cavity in the temporal bone of the skull with a system of passages comprising two main functional parts: The inner ear is found in all vertebrates, with substantial variations in form and function.
Generated Simplification
The inner ear is a part of the body found in all animals with backbones, including humans. It has different shapes and functions depending on the species. In humans and other mammals, the inner ear is located inside the skull. It is made up of a hollow space in a bone called the temporal bone. This space is called the "bony labyrinth" and contains a system of connected passages. If you have questions about the inner ear or its role in your health, talk to your doctor.
Reference Simplification
In mammals, it consists of two main working parts inside a bony labyrinth: The inner ear is found in all vertebrates, with many variations in form and function.

The jaundice usually peaks in 1 to 2 weeks, then fades over 2 to 4 weeks.

Example 231 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 231
Source Text
Myocardial abnormalities occur in about 8 % of patients within weeks of erythema migrans.
Generated Simplification
Heart problems can happen in about 8% of people within a few weeks after developing erythema migrans. Erythema migrans is a type of rash that often appears as a sign of Lyme disease, which is caused by a tick bite. If you have had erythema migrans, it is important to watch for any unusual symptoms, especially those related to your heart, such as chest pain, shortness of breath, or feeling faint. If you notice any of these symptoms, contact your doctor right away. You should also discuss any concerns about your heart health with your healthcare provider.

Up to 8 % of infected people develop heart problems.

Example 235 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 235
Source Text
Among spinal subdural abscesses, methicillin-sensitive Staphylococcus aureus is the most common organism involved. Rarely parasites can cause abscesses and this is more common in the developing world.
Generated Simplification
Spinal subdural abscesses are infections that occur in the space around the spinal cord. - The most common cause of these infections is a type of bacteria called methicillin-sensitive Staphylococcus aureus (MSSA). - In rare cases, parasites can also cause these abscesses. This is more likely to happen in developing countries. If you have concerns about spinal infections or symptoms like back pain, fever, or weakness, talk to your healthcare provider.
Reference Simplification
Among spinal subdural abscesses, methicillin-sensitive Staphylococcus aureus is the most common organism involved.

Example 236 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 236
Source Text
Periungual warts are more common among patients who bite their nails or who have occupations where their hands are chronically wet such as dishwashers and bartenders. Genital warts manifest as discrete flat to broad-based smooth to velvety papules to rough and pedunculated excrescences on the perineal, perirectal, labial, and penile areas. Perirectal warts often itch.
Generated Simplification
### Periungual Warts - Periungual warts are warts that grow around the fingernails. - They are more common in people who bite their nails. - Jobs that keep hands wet for long periods, like dishwashing or bartending, can also increase the risk of these warts. ### Genital Warts - Genital warts can appear on areas like the genitals, around the anus, or nearby skin. - They may look different from person to person. Some are flat and smooth, while others are rough or have a velvety texture. - In some cases, they may be raised and have a stalk-like appearance. - Warts around the anus (perirectal warts) often cause itching. ### What You Should Do - If you notice any unusual growths or warts, talk to your healthcare provider. - Let them know if you have itching or other symptoms. - They can help diagnose the type of wart and recommend treatment if needed.
Reference Simplification
Also called venereal warts or condylomata acuminata, genital warts occur on the penis, anus, vulva, vagina, and cervix. They can be flat, smooth, and velvety or irregular, bumpy growths often with the texture of a small cauliflower. Warts around the anus often itch.

Example 238 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 238
Source Text
The risk of overdose is increased if flunitrazepam is taken in combination with CNS depressants such as alcohol and opiates.
Generated Simplification
Taking flunitrazepam with certain substances can increase the risk of an overdose. - These substances include alcohol and opiates. - Both alcohol and opiates affect the central nervous system (CNS), which controls important body functions like breathing. If you are using flunitrazepam, avoid drinking alcohol or taking opiates unless your doctor has told you it is safe. Talk to your healthcare provider if you have questions or concerns about this risk.
Reference Simplification
The overdose will be worse if flunitrazepam is taken with depressants, like alcohol or opiates.

Example 239 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 239
Source Text
The Intersex Society of North America stated that `` For decades, doctors have thought it necessary to treat intersex with a concealment-centered approach, one that features downplaying intersex as much as possible, even to the point of lying to patients about their conditions.
Generated Simplification
Doctors have often used a "concealment-centered approach" to treat intersex conditions. This means they tried to hide or minimize the condition as much as possible. In some cases, this even included not fully informing patients about their own medical situation.
Reference Simplification
About 1 in 100 babies are born with an intersex condition, and 1 in 1000 babies have an intersex condition that the medical community declares needs surgery, in order adhere to binary sex/gender ideals.

Example 240 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 240
Source Text
Biopsy of the lung is usually not indicated unless the pulmonary hypertension is thought to be due to an underlying interstitial lung disease; further, lung biopsies are fraught with risks of bleeding due to the high intrapulmonary blood pressure.
Generated Simplification
A lung biopsy is not usually needed unless doctors think the pulmonary hypertension (high blood pressure in the lungs) is caused by another lung condition, such as interstitial lung disease. Lung biopsies can be risky because they may cause bleeding. This is due to the high blood pressure in the lungs. If your doctor has mentioned a lung biopsy, it is important to discuss the risks and benefits with them.
Reference Simplification
Biopsy of the lung is usually not indicated unless the pulmonary hypertension is thought to be due to an underlying interstitial lung disease.

Example 241 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 241
Source Text
Epidemiological studies can only go to prove that an agent could have caused, but not that it did cause, an effect in any particular case: In United States law, epidemiology alone can not prove that a causal association does not exist in general.
Generated Simplification
Epidemiological studies help us understand if something *could* cause a certain effect. However, they cannot prove that it *did* cause the effect in a specific person. In the United States, these studies alone cannot prove that there is no link between a cause and an effect for everyone.
Reference Simplification
Epidemiological studies can only go to prove that an agent could have caused disease in population, but not that it did cause, an effect in any particular case.

Example 242 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 242
Source Text
Signs occur along a continuum: no changes to varicose veins (rare) to edema to stasis dermatitis on the lower legs and at the ankles, with or without ulceration (see Table: Clinical Classification of Chronic Venous Insufficiency). Venous stasis dermatitis consists of reddish brown hyperpigmentation, induration, venous ectasia, lipodermatosclerosis (fibrosing subcutaneous panniculitis), and venous stasis ulcers.
Generated Simplification
### Understanding Venous Stasis and Its Signs Venous stasis refers to problems with blood flow in the veins, often in the legs. It can cause a range of symptoms, from mild to severe. Here’s what you should know: #### Signs and Symptoms - **No visible changes**: In rare cases, there may be no noticeable symptoms. - **Swelling (edema)**: This is a common early sign, especially in the lower legs and ankles. - **Skin changes (stasis dermatitis)**: This includes: - Reddish-brown discoloration of the skin. - Thickened or hardened skin (induration). - Enlarged veins (venous ectasia). - Inflammation and scarring under the skin (lipodermatosclerosis). - Open sores or wounds (venous stasis ulcers), which may or may not be present. #### What This Means for You If you notice swelling, skin discoloration, or sores on your legs or ankles, it could be a sign of venous stasis. These symptoms may get worse over time if not treated. #### Next Steps - Talk to your healthcare provider if you have any of these symptoms. They can help diagnose the problem and recommend treatment. - Early treatment can help prevent complications, such as ulcers or further skin damage. If you have questions or concerns about your symptoms, make sure to bring them up during your next medical visit.
Reference Simplification
The discoloration is caused by red blood cells that escape from swollen (distended) veins into the skin. The discolored skin is vulnerable, and even a minor injury, such as that from scratching or a bump, can break it open, resulting in an ulcer.

Plasmids are small, circular pieces of DNA. They are usually found in bacteria. Sometimes, they can also be found in other organisms, like archaea and eukaryotes.

Source Text
Early liver metastases may be asymptomatic.
Generated Simplification
### Understanding Early Liver Metastases - Early liver metastases, which are cancer cells that have spread to the liver from another part of the body, may not cause any symptoms. - This means you might not feel sick or notice any changes in your body at first. ### What This Means for You - Regular check-ups and tests are important if you are at risk for liver metastases or have a history of cancer. - If you have concerns or questions about your risk, talk to your healthcare provider. They can guide you on what to watch for and whether any tests are needed.

Often, the first symptoms are vague.

Example 245 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 245
Source Text
A common finding during physical examination is a symmetry or a dominant mass âa mass distinctly different from the surrounding breast tissue. Diffuse fibrotic changes in a quadrant of the breast, usually the upper outer quadrant, are more characteristic of benign disorders; a slightly firmer thickening in one breast but not the other may be a sign of cancer.
Generated Simplification
During a physical exam, doctors may notice changes in the breast. Here are some key points to understand: - A lump or mass that feels different from the surrounding breast tissue is a common finding. - If there are widespread fibrous (thickened) changes in one area of the breast, especially in the upper outer part, this is often linked to non-cancerous (benign) conditions. - However, if one breast feels slightly firmer or thicker in one specific area compared to the other breast, it could be a sign of cancer. If you notice any of these changes in your breasts, it is important to talk to your doctor. They can help determine what the changes mean and if further tests are needed.
Reference Simplification
Such a lump may be cancer if it is a firm, distinctive thickening that appears in one breast but not the other. Usually, scattered lumpy changes in the breast, especially the upper outer region, are not cancerous and indicate fibrocystic changes.

Example 246 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 246
Source Text
More advanced breast cancers are characterized by one or more of the following: Fixation of the mass to the chest wall or to overlying skin; Satellite nodules or ulcers in the skin; Exaggeration of the usual skin markings resulting from skin edema caused by invasion of dermal lymphatic vessels (so-called peau d â orange).
Generated Simplification
Advanced breast cancer can have certain signs. These may include: - A lump in the breast that is stuck to the chest wall or the skin above it. - Small lumps or sores on the skin near the main lump. - Skin changes, such as swelling that makes the skin look dimpled, like an orange peel. This happens when cancer affects tiny lymph vessels in the skin. If you notice any of these changes, talk to your doctor right away. They can help determine what is happening and discuss next steps.
Reference Simplification
In more advanced stages, the lump usually adheres to the chest wall or the skin over it. If a breast contains cancer that adheres to the chest wall or skin, this maneuver may make the skin pucker or dimple or make one breast appear different from the other. In very advanced cancer, swollen bumps or festering sores may develop on the skin. Sometimes the skin over the lump is dimpled and leathery and looks like the skin of an orange (peau d â orange) except in color.

Two leading hypotheses suggest it may have jumped species from the disease scrapie in sheep, or that it evolved from a spontaneous form of `` mad cow disease '' that has been seen occasionally in cattle for many centuries.

Scientists believe there are two main ideas about how this disease started in cows: - It might have come from a disease in sheep called scrapie. - It could have developed on its own from a rare condition in cows, sometimes called "mad cow disease," which has been seen in cattle for hundreds of years. If you have questions about this, talk to your doctor.

Example 248 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 248
Source Text
Myoclonus that occurs when patients are suddenly startled (startle myoclonus) may be an early symptom of Creutzfeldt-Jacob disease.
Generated Simplification
### Startle Myoclonus and Creutzfeldt-Jakob Disease - Myoclonus refers to sudden, quick muscle jerks. - Startle myoclonus happens when these jerks occur after a sudden surprise or startle. This type of myoclonus can sometimes be an early sign of a rare brain condition called Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease (CJD). ### What You Should Do - If you or someone you know experiences frequent or unusual muscle jerks, especially when startled, talk to a healthcare professional. - A doctor can evaluate the symptoms and decide if further tests are needed. CJD is a serious condition, so early evaluation is important.
Reference Simplification
In Creutzfeldt-Jacob disease (a rare degenerative brain disorder), myoclonus becomes more obvious when people are suddenly startled.

Example 250 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 250
Source Text
Vaccinia given after exposure to smallpox, within the first three days, is reported to attenuate the disease considerably, and vaccination up to a week after exposure probably offers some protection from disease or may modify the severity of disease.
Generated Simplification
If you are exposed to smallpox, getting the vaccinia vaccine quickly can help. - If you receive the vaccine within the first three days after exposure, it can significantly reduce the severity of the disease. - If you get the vaccine up to seven days after exposure, it may still provide some protection or make the illness less severe. If you think you have been exposed to smallpox, contact a healthcare professional immediately to discuss vaccination.
Reference Simplification
This same method using liquid from sores was also used to give people smallpox.

Example 251 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 251
Source Text
Urinary catheterization increases the risk for urinary tract infections.
Generated Simplification
Using a urinary catheter can raise the chance of getting a urinary tract infection (UTI). If you have a catheter, it is important to watch for signs of infection, such as burning, pain, fever, or cloudy urine. Talk to your healthcare provider if you notice these symptoms or have concerns about your catheter. Your doctor can also explain ways to lower the risk of infection.
Reference Simplification
Women get urinary tract infections more often than men.

Example 253 | Profile: older_adults | Language: English | ID/Index: 253
Source Text
Because patients lack the motor responses (eg, withdrawal from painful stimuli) usually used to measure responsiveness, they may be mistakenly thought to be unconscious. Thus, all patients who can not move should have their comprehension tested by requesting eye blinking or vertical eye movements.
Generated Simplification
Sometimes, patients who cannot move may seem unconscious because they do not show usual reactions, like pulling away from pain. However, they might still be aware. To check if a patient understands, doctors can ask them to: - Blink their eyes. - Move their eyes up or down. If you or someone you care for cannot move, talk to a healthcare professional about how to test for understanding.
Reference Simplification
Because locked-in syndrome can be mistaken for stupor or coma, doctors test people who do not move and appear unresponsive by asking them to open and close their eyes.
